title: ficus benguetensis f. leytensis
intro: Ficus benguetensis f. leytensis, also known as the Philippine forest ficus, is a rare and endemic species of fig tree native to the Philippines. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, particularly respiratory issues, thanks to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cough, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular remedy for respiratory problems. Ficus benguetensis f. leytensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to be made for medicinal use.
